title: plantago media subsp. brutia
intro: Plantago media subsp. brutia, also known as broad-leaved plantain, is a subspecies of the plantain plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is also known to have a number of health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Plantago media subsp. brutia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
